an example : Let the Sun's position be 8s-3°-29'-37'i'
and the Moon's 3s-20°-26'-20". By subtracting the former from the latter
we get 7s-16°-56'-43" By reducing it to degrees we get 227 roughly. As
this is more than 180°, it is the dark fortnight. Now divide this by i 2"
and find out the lunar day : It is 18 and plus something. That means
thai it is the 19th Tithi or the 4th (caturthi) in the dark fortnight. For our
purpose in this particular case, however, you have to calculate the Tithi
from the product mentioned above. In this case what you have to remember
is that there are certain lunar days that are called "Chidra" or unholy. They
are the 4th, ftth, 8th, 9th, 12th and 14th lunar days in both the halves.
2. Karana means a half-lunar day. There are altogether II Karanas
distributed among the 30 Tithis in a lunar month at the rate of 2 Karanas
for each Tithi. Out of the 11 Karanas four viz. (1) Catu;p3d, (2) Naga,
